Name: HSP70
Inducer_Tissue_Trigger: Heat shock (42C) / heavy metals / oxidative stress

Name: GAL1
Inducer_Tissue_Trigger: Extremely Strong Induction with Galactose (repressed by glucose)

Name: T7
Inducer_Tissue_Trigger: (Presence of T7 RNA polymerase) ONLY WORKS IN BACTERIA WITH T7 RNA POLYMERASE. Most strains of T7 Ecoli are engineered to make T7 RNA Polymerase under exposure of IPTG.
Usable_In: E. coli BL21(DE3) / T7 RNAP strains

Name: Ptac
Inducer_Tissue_Trigger: (IPTG) isopropyl-beta-d-thiogalactopyranoside, but ensure the bacterial strain already expresses LAC operon for proper inducibility

Name: araBAD
Inducer_Tissue_Trigger: Arabinose (repressed by glucose) (Host organism must contain arabidose operon and AraC for proper function)

Name: Ptet
Inducer_Tissue_Trigger: Anhydrotetracycline (aTc) (Ensure host organism creates TetR repressor for proper function)

Name: Plux
Inducer_Tissue_Trigger: N-acyl homoserine lactone (AHL) + (host must produce luxR repressor. In V. fischeri, this promoter will only activate if the bacterium senses its in a quorum)

Name: PrhaB
Inducer_Tissue_Trigger: L-Rhamnose (repressed by glucose), (host must already have rhaBAD operon for proper function)

Name: cspA
Inducer_Tissue_Trigger: Cold shock temperature downshift to 15 degrees C

Name: EM7
Inducer_Tissue_Trigger: (very stable and always works regardless of E. coli strain)

Name: pSpac-hy
Inducer_Tissue_Trigger: IPTG (requires host bacteria to have lac operon)

Name: paraBAD (AND)
Inducer_Tissue_Trigger: AraC protein PLUS CRP-cAMP complex BOTH required for expression
